items.title: Migration adds model + cli_session_id to sessions
items.expected: Migration 0005_session_model applies; sessions has nullable model and cli_session_id columns.
items.evidence: Applied during execution: `Batch 3 run: 1 migrations`. Confirmed at runtime: POST /api/sessions {model:'haiku'} returned a row with model='haiku'.

items.title: Slash commands are interpreted over stream-json stdin
steps: 1. Pipe a stream-json user message of `/context`, then `/compact`.
items.expected: /context returns the context report; /compact handled as a command.
items.evidence: Probe: /context returned 'Tokens: 64k / 1m (6%)'; /compact returned 'Not enough messages to compact.'

items.title: OAuth usage endpoint returns 5h + weekly windows
items.precondition: Subscription OAuth token in the macOS keychain.
items.expected: five_hour and seven_day with utilization + resets_at.
items.evidence: Live: HTTP 200, five_hour util 54%, seven_day util 21%, with reset timestamps.

items.title: GET /api/usage/limits returns the envelope without the token
items.expected: { success:true, data:{ limits:{ available, fiveHour, sevenDay, fetchedAt } } } — no token.
items.evidence: 200: {available:true, fiveHour:{utilization:54,resetsAt:...}, sevenDay:{utilization:21,resetsAt:...}, fetchedAt:...}. grep for accessToken/Bearer/eyJ/oauth in payload: none.

items.title: Sidebar usage ring data (5h + weekly + reset)
items.expected: Two windows with utilization + reset; '—' when unavailable.
items.evidence: Data path verified end-to-end: /api/usage/limits returns live utilization + resetsAt that the ring/dropdown render. SVG component is presentational and tsc-clean; pixels not separately screenshotted (no headless Electron harness).
items.notes: Visual render relies on the verified data contract.

items.title: Context ring data rises as the session fills its window
steps: 1. Drive turns over the WS; observe `context` events.
items.expected: used/window track context growth per model.
items.evidence: WS drive: context event haiku used=57865/window=200000 (29%), then sonnet used=77594/window=1000000 (8%). ContextRing renders this presentational data (tsc-clean); pixels not separately screenshotted.
items.notes: Window correctly reflects the active model (200k haiku vs 1M sonnet).

items.title: Switching model mid-chat preserves conversation context
items.precondition: An active session with a prior turn.
steps: 1. Turn 1: plant codeword ZEBRA-42 on model haiku.
3. Turn 2: ask to recall the codeword.
items.expected: Model label updates to sonnet; answer shows context was retained.
items.evidence: WS drive: model_updated/capabilities -> claude-sonnet-5 after switch; turn 2 replied 'ZEBRA-42' — resume preserved context across the restart. Driven through the real WS surface the UI uses.
items.notes: Picker disabled while streaming; backend rejects mid-turn switch (SESSION_BUSY).

items.title: Skill autocomplete source and /-prefixed send
items.precondition: An active session whose init reported slash commands.
steps: 1. capabilities event supplies the autocomplete list.
items.evidence: capabilities event delivered 32 slash commands + 17 skills over the WS (autocomplete source). /-send path is the same verified user_turn path; slash-over-stdin confirmed by T2-probe. Dropdown pixels not separately screenshotted.

items.title: /compact control compacts the conversation
steps: 1. The button sends /compact via the verified user_turn path.
items.expected: Claude compacts context (or reports too few messages).
items.evidence: 'compact' present in the session's 32 slash commands; CLI-level behavior confirmed by T2-probe; button reuses the verified send path.

items.title: OAuth token never leaks to client or logs
items.precondition: App running with usage polling active.
items.expected: No access token in the response.
items.evidence: grep of the live payload for accessToken/Bearer/sk-ant/eyJ/oauth: no match. Token confined to ClaudeUsageService; only utilization/reset cross the boundary.
items.notes: Server-log redaction relies on the service never logging the token (code review); not separately grepped at runtime.
